samples.prompt: Analyze this Boeing 747-400 commercial airliner. Describe the aircraft's wing configuration, engine placement, and identify the specific variant features visible in this image.
samples.text: The Boeing 747-400 is a wide-body, four-engine jet airliner. It features a distinctive upper deck hump, high-bypass turbofan engines mounted on pylons under swept wings, and a conventional tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer.
samples.vfa_target: Aircraft aerodynamics and commercial aviation engineering

questions.text: What type of engines does the Boeing 747-400 typically use?
questions.q: What type of engines does the Boeing 747-400 typically use?

questions.text: What is the typical cruise altitude for a Boeing 747-400?
questions.q: What is the typical cruise altitude for a Boeing 747-400?

samples.prompt: Examine this Airbus A380. What aerodynamic features are visible? Describe the wing design, engine configuration, and how this double-decker aircraft achieves its lift characteristics.
samples.text: The Airbus A380 is a full-length double-deck, wide-body, four-engine jet. It features swept wings with a high aspect ratio, winglets for fuel efficiency, and four large turbofan engines. The aircraft's massive size requires reinforced landing gear with 22 wheels.
samples.vfa_target: Large aircraft aerodynamics and structural engineering

questions.text: How many wheels does the Airbus A380 landing gear have?
questions.q: How many wheels does the Airbus A380 landing gear have?

questions.text: What is the wingspan of the Airbus A380?
questions.q: What is the wingspan of the Airbus A380?

samples.prompt: Analyze this Space Shuttle. Describe the thermal protection system, solid rocket boosters, and how the vehicle transitions from launch to orbital flight to reentry.
samples.text: The Space Shuttle was a partially reusable low Earth orbital spacecraft system. It featured thermal protection tiles, two solid rocket boosters, an external tank, and three main engines. The vehicle launched vertically and landed horizontally as a glider.
samples.vfa_target: Spacecraft engineering and orbital mechanics

questions.text: What material were the Space Shuttle's thermal protection tiles made of?
questions.q: What material were the Space Shuttle's thermal protection tiles made of?

questions.text: How many Space Shuttle missions were flown in total?
questions.q: How many Space Shuttle missions were flown in total?

samples.prompt: Examine this Boeing CH-47 Chinook helicopter. Describe its tandem rotor configuration, the advantages of this design, and how it achieves vertical lift and directional control.
samples.text: The Boeing CH-47 Chinook is a tandem-rotor, heavy-lift helicopter. Its dual rotor configuration eliminates the need for a tail rotor, providing superior lifting capacity and stability. The counter-rotating rotors cancel out torque effects.
samples.vfa_target: Helicopter aerodynamics and rotorcraft engineering

questions.text: What is the main advantage of tandem rotor configuration?
questions.q: What is the main advantage of tandem rotor configuration?

questions.text: What is the maximum payload capacity of the CH-47 Chinook?
questions.q: What is the maximum payload capacity of the CH-47 Chinook?

samples.prompt: Analyze this Gulfstream G650 business jet. Describe its swept wing design, engine configuration, and how the aircraft achieves its long-range cruise efficiency at high altitude.
samples.text: The Gulfstream G650 is a large-cabin, ultra-long-range business jet. It features swept wings with winglets, two Rolls-Royce BR725 turbofan engines, and a fly-by-wire flight control system. The aircraft cruises at Mach 0.85-0.90 at altitudes up to 51,000 feet.
samples.vfa_target: Business aviation and long-range aircraft design

questions.text: What is the maximum cruise speed of the Gulfstream G650?
questions.q: What is the maximum cruise speed of the Gulfstream G650?

questions.text: What engines power the Gulfstream G650?
questions.q: What engines power the Gulfstream G650?

samples.prompt: Analyze this container cargo ship. Describe its hull design, propulsion system, and how modern container ships achieve fuel efficiency while carrying thousands of TEU containers.
samples.text: Modern container cargo ships feature bulbous bows for reduced hydrodynamic resistance, large diesel engines for propulsion, and stacked container arrangements above and below deck. These vessels can carry 20,000+ TEU containers across oceans.
samples.vfa_target: Marine engineering and naval architecture

questions.text: What does TEU stand for in container shipping?
questions.q: What does TEU stand for in container shipping?

questions.text: What is the purpose of a bulbous bow on cargo ships?
questions.q: What is the purpose of a bulbous bow on cargo ships?

samples.prompt: Examine this Caterpillar 797F mining haul truck. Describe its massive size, suspension system, and how it operates in open-pit mining environments to transport ore and overburden.
samples.text: The Caterpillar 797F is one of the world's largest mining haul trucks. It features a payload capacity of 400 tons, hydraulic suspension, and a diesel-electric powertrain. The truck operates in extreme conditions in open-pit mines worldwide.
samples.vfa_target: Heavy equipment engineering and mining operations

questions.text: What is the payload capacity of the Caterpillar 797F?
questions.q: What is the payload capacity of the Caterpillar 797F?

questions.text: What type of powertrain does the 797F use?
questions.q: What type of powertrain does the 797F use?

samples.prompt: Analyze this tower crane at a construction site. Describe its jib configuration, counterweight system, and how it lifts heavy loads to significant heights while maintaining stability.
samples.text: Tower cranes feature a horizontal jib, counter-jib with counterweights, and a vertical tower structure. They use a trolley mechanism to move loads along the jib and can lift loads of 20+ tons to heights exceeding 200 meters.
samples.vfa_target: Structural engineering and construction equipment

questions.text: What provides stability for a tower crane?
questions.q: What provides stability for a tower crane?

questions.text: What mechanism moves loads horizontally on a tower crane?
questions.q: What mechanism moves loads horizontally on a tower crane?

samples.prompt: Examine this hydraulic excavator. Describe its boom, arm, and bucket configuration, hydraulic system, and how it achieves precise digging and material handling in construction and mining applications.
samples.text: Hydraulic excavators feature a boom, arm, and bucket connected by hydraulic cylinders. The hydraulic system provides the force needed for digging, while the operator controls precise movements through joysticks and pedals.
samples.vfa_target: Hydraulic systems and construction equipment engineering

questions.q: What provides the digging force in a hydraulic excavator?

questions.q: What is the typical operating weight of a large mining excavator?

samples.prompt: Analyze this heavy haul truck used for oversized cargo transport. Describe its multi-axle configuration, steering system, and how it manages weight distribution for transporting extremely heavy loads.
samples.text: Heavy haul trucks feature multiple axles for weight distribution, hydraulic steering systems, and powerful diesel engines. These vehicles transport oversized cargo like transformers, generators, and industrial equipment.

questions.text: Why do heavy haul trucks have multiple axles?
questions.q: Why do heavy haul trucks have multiple axles?

questions.q: What type of loads do heavy haul trucks typically transport?

samples.prompt: Analyze this industrial robot arm in a manufacturing setting. Describe its joint configuration, degrees of freedom, and how it achieves precise positioning for assembly tasks.
samples.text: Industrial robot arms typically feature 6 degrees of freedom, allowing them to reach any position and orientation in their workspace. They use servo motors and encoders for precise positioning and can perform tasks like welding, painting, and assembly.
samples.vfa_target: Robotics engineering and automation systems

questions.q: How many degrees of freedom does a typical industrial robot arm have?

questions.q: What sensors provide position feedback in robot arms?

samples.prompt: Examine this autonomous mobile robot in a warehouse setting. Describe its navigation system, sensor suite, and how it navigates dynamic environments while avoiding obstacles.
samples.text: Autonomous mobile robots use LiDAR, cameras, and ultrasonic sensors for navigation. They employ simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) algorithms to build maps and track their position in real-time.

samples.prompt: Analyze this collaborative robot (cobot) working alongside human operators. Describe its safety features, force-limited joints, and how it achieves safe human-robot interaction.
samples.text: Collaborative robots feature force-limited joints, rounded edges, and proximity sensors to ensure safe human-robot interaction. They can stop or yield when detecting contact, making them suitable for shared workspaces.

questions.text: What is the main safety feature of collaborative robots?
questions.q: What is the main safety feature of collaborative robots?

questions.q: What distinguishes cobots from traditional industrial robots?

samples.prompt: Analyze this CNC machining center. Describe its tool changer system, spindle configuration, and how computer numerical control achieves precise material removal in manufacturing.
samples.text: CNC machining centers feature automatic tool changers, high-speed spindles, and computer-controlled axes. They read G-code instructions to precisely remove material from workpieces, achieving tolerances of ±0.001 inches.
samples.vfa_target: Precision manufacturing and CNC technology

samples.prompt: Examine this industrial 3D printer. Describe its additive manufacturing process, build chamber, and how it creates complex geometries layer by layer using various materials.
samples.text: Industrial 3D printers use additive manufacturing to build objects layer by layer. They can process metals, plastics, and ceramics, creating complex geometries impossible with traditional manufacturing methods.

samples.prompt: Analyze this automated assembly line in an automotive factory. Describe the conveyor system, robotic workstations, and how the line achieves high throughput with consistent quality.
samples.text: Automated assembly lines use conveyors to move products through sequential workstations. Robots perform repetitive tasks like welding and fastening, while quality control stations ensure consistency.

samples.prompt: Analyze this offshore oil platform in the North Sea. Describe its structural design, drilling equipment, and how it withstands harsh marine conditions while extracting hydrocarbons.
samples.text: Offshore oil platforms feature jacket structures anchored to the seabed, drilling derricks, and processing facilities. They withstand extreme waves, winds, and currents while extracting oil and gas from subsea reservoirs.
samples.vfa_target: Offshore engineering and petroleum extraction

questions.text: What type of structure is commonly used for offshore platforms in shallow water?
questions.q: What type of structure is commonly used for offshore platforms in shallow water?

questions.q: What is the maximum depth for fixed offshore platforms?

samples.prompt: Examine this modern wind turbine. Describe its rotor design, gearbox system, and how it converts wind energy into electrical power through electromagnetic induction.
samples.text: Modern wind turbines feature three-blade rotors connected to gearboxes and generators. The gearbox increases rotational speed from 10-20 RPM to 1,500-1,800 RPM for the generator.
samples.vfa_target: Renewable energy engineering and wind power

questions.text: How many blades do modern wind turbines typically have?
questions.q: How many blades do modern wind turbines typically have?

questions.text: What does the gearbox do in a wind turbine?
questions.q: What does the gearbox do in a wind turbine?

samples.prompt: Analyze this utility-scale solar photovoltaic farm. Describe the panel configuration, tracking systems, and how it converts sunlight into grid-compatible electrical power.
samples.text: Utility-scale solar farms use thousands of photovoltaic panels mounted on tracking systems. Single-axis trackers follow the sun's east-west movement, increasing energy yield by 25-30% compared to fixed installations.
samples.vfa_target: Solar energy engineering and grid integration

questions.text: What do solar tracking systems do?
questions.q: What do solar tracking systems do?

questions.text: How much more energy do tracking systems produce vs fixed panels?
questions.q: How much more energy do tracking systems produce vs fixed panels?

samples.prompt: Analyze this printed circuit board (PCB). Describe its layer stackup, component placement, and how surface mount technology enables high-density electronic assembly.
samples.text: Modern PCBs feature multiple layers of copper traces, surface mount components, and via connections between layers. SMT allows components as small as 0201 packages (0.6mm x 0.3mm) to be placed automatically.
samples.vfa_target: Electronic packaging and PCB design

questions.text: What does SMT stand for in electronics manufacturing?
questions.q: What does SMT stand for in electronics manufacturing?

questions.text: What is the purpose of vias in a PCB?
questions.q: What is the purpose of vias in a PCB?

samples.prompt: Examine this semiconductor silicon wafer. Describe the photolithography process, transistor structures, and how billions of transistors are fabricated on a single chip.
samples.text: Semiconductor fabrication uses photolithography to pattern transistors on silicon wafers. Modern processes achieve feature sizes below 7nm, allowing billions of transistors on a single chip.
samples.vfa_target: Semiconductor fabrication and nanotechnology

questions.text: What process patterns transistors on silicon wafers?
questions.q: What process patterns transistors on silicon wafers?

questions.text: What is the smallest feature size in modern chip fabrication?
questions.q: What is the smallest feature size in modern chip fabrication?

samples.prompt: Analyze this advanced semiconductor package. Describe the lead frame, wire bonding, and how the package provides electrical connections and thermal management for the die.
samples.text: Semiconductor packages protect the die and provide electrical connections to the PCB. Wire bonds or flip-chip bumps connect the die to the lead frame, while thermal pads dissipate heat.
samples.vfa_target: Electronic packaging and thermal management

questions.text: What connects the die to the lead frame in wire bonding?
questions.q: What connects the die to the lead frame in wire bonding?

questions.text: What is the main purpose of semiconductor packaging?
questions.q: What is the main purpose of semiconductor packaging?

samples.prompt: Analyze this Boeing 747 during a nose-up climb. Describe the aircraft's pitch angle, wing loading during climb, and how the high-lift devices (flaps and slats) are configured for this climb attitude.
samples.text: During a nose-up climb, the Boeing 747 increases its angle of attack, generating additional lift from the wing's camber. The leading-edge slats and trailing-edge flaps may be partially deployed at lower altitudes. The four turbofan engines provide excess thrust to maintain the climb rate while the fuselage attitude tilts upward relative to the horizon.
samples.vfa_target: Aircraft climb dynamics and high-lift aerodynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent upward pitch angle, fuselage oscillates or drifts level mid-generation

questions.text: What is the typical climb angle for a Boeing 747 after takeoff?
questions.q: What is the typical climb angle for a Boeing 747 after takeoff?

questions.text: What happens to induced drag during a steep climb?
questions.q: What happens to induced drag during a steep climb?

questions.text: Which control surface primarily controls pitch attitude?
questions.q: Which control surface primarily controls pitch attitude?

questions.text: What is the relationship between angle of attack and lift in the linear range?
questions.q: What is the relationship between angle of attack and lift in the linear range?

samples.prompt: Analyze this offshore wind farm from a vertical rise perspective. Describe the turbine spacing, foundation types, and how the array layout optimizes energy capture while minimizing wake interference between turbines.
samples.text: Offshore wind farms use monopile or jacket foundations anchored to the seabed. Turbines are spaced 5-9 rotor diameters apart to minimize wake losses. The vertical rise viewpoint reveals the staggered array layout designed to maximize energy capture from prevailing wind directions while reducing turbulence from upstream turbines.
samples.vfa_target: Offshore wind farm layout and wake interaction dynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model distorts turbine vertical proportions during upward camera rise, towers appear to stretch or compress unevenly

questions.text: What is the typical spacing between offshore wind turbines?
questions.q: What is the typical spacing between offshore wind turbines?

questions.text: What is a monopile foundation used for in offshore wind?
questions.q: What is a monopile foundation used for in offshore wind?

questions.text: What is wake interference in a wind farm?
questions.q: What is wake interference in a wind farm?

samples.prompt: Analyze this conveyor belt system from a side view. Describe the belt material, roller configuration, drive mechanism, and how the system maintains consistent tension and speed for material transport.
samples.text: Industrial conveyor belts use vulcanized rubber or PVC belts supported by idler rollers spaced at regular intervals. The drive pulley at the head provides traction through friction, while gravity take-up mechanisms maintain belt tension. Speed is controlled by variable frequency drives (VFDs) connected to the motor and gearbox assembly.
samples.vfa_target: Conveyor kinematics and material handling dynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to render consistent belt motion, rollers appear to rotate out of sync or belt surface texture shifts during translation

questions.text: What maintains tension in a conveyor belt system?
questions.q: What maintains tension in a conveyor belt system?

questions.text: What device controls conveyor belt speed?
questions.q: What device controls conveyor belt speed?

questions.text: What provides traction between the drive pulley and belt?
questions.q: What provides traction between the drive pulley and belt?

questions.text: What is the typical spacing of idler rollers on a conveyor?
questions.q: What is the typical spacing of idler rollers on a conveyor?

samples.prompt: Analyze this assembly line with a robotic arm performing kinematic operations. Describe the robot's joint movements, end-effector configuration, and how the arm coordinates with the moving assembly line to perform tasks on parts in motion.
samples.text: Assembly line robotic arms use synchronized motion control to track parts on a moving conveyor. The robot's trajectory planner computes inverse kinematics solutions in real-time, adjusting joint angles to maintain the end-effector position relative to the moving workpiece. Force-torque sensors at the wrist provide feedback for precise assembly operations.
samples.vfa_target: Robotic kinematics and synchronized assembly automation
samples.failure_target: I2V model desynchronizes robot arm motion from conveyor speed, end-effector drifts away from workpiece during generation

questions.text: What does inverse kinematics compute in robotics?
questions.q: What does inverse kinematics compute in robotics?

questions.text: What sensor provides force feedback at a robot wrist?
questions.q: What sensor provides force feedback at a robot wrist?

questions.text: How do robots track parts on a moving conveyor?
questions.q: How do robots track parts on a moving conveyor?

samples.prompt: Analyze this PCB during a dolly-in zoom toward a specific component cluster. Describe the surface mount components, solder joint geometry, trace routing, and how the zoom reveals progressively finer details of the circuit board construction.
samples.text: A dolly-in zoom on a PCB reveals layers of detail: from the macro layout of component placement and trace routing, through individual SMD packages with their pad geometries, down to solder fillet profiles and via structures. The depth of field narrows as the camera approaches, isolating the target components from the surrounding board.
samples.vfa_target: PCB macro-to-micro structural detail and component inspection
samples.failure_target: I2V model loses fine detail during dolly-in zoom, solder joints blur or trace patterns become inconsistent at closer viewpoints

questions.text: What happens to depth of field during a dolly-in zoom?
questions.q: What happens to depth of field during a dolly-in zoom?

questions.text: What is the smallest common SMD package size?
questions.q: What is the smallest common SMD package size?

questions.text: What connects traces between PCB layers?
questions.q: What connects traces between PCB layers?

questions.text: What is a solder fillet?
questions.q: What is a solder fillet?

samples.prompt: Analyze this heavy truck engine bay during a dolly-in zoom. Describe the diesel engine components, cooling system layout, turbocharger placement, and how the progressive zoom reveals the mechanical complexity of the powertrain.
samples.text: A dolly-in zoom on a heavy truck engine bay reveals the turbocharged diesel engine with its inline configuration, charge air cooler, and serpentine belt system. The progressive approach exposes details from the overall engine layout to individual fuel injector lines, wiring harnesses, and the turbocharger compressor housing.
samples.vfa_target: Heavy vehicle powertrain architecture and engine component detail
samples.failure_target: I2V model introduces spatial distortion during dolly-in, engine components shift relative positions or turbocharger geometry distorts

questions.text: What is the purpose of a charge air cooler in a diesel engine?
questions.q: What is the purpose of a charge air cooler in a diesel engine?

questions.text: What drives the turbocharger in a diesel engine?
questions.q: What drives the turbocharger in a diesel engine?

questions.text: What is the typical configuration of heavy truck diesel engines?
questions.q: What is the typical configuration of heavy truck diesel engines?

samples.prompt: Analyze this wind turbine with spinning blades. Describe the blade pitch control, rotational dynamics, and how the generator converts the mechanical rotation into electrical power through the gearbox and generator assembly.
samples.text: Wind turbine blades rotate at 10-20 RPM, with pitch actuators adjusting blade angle to optimize energy capture. The low-speed shaft drives through a planetary gearbox to the high-speed generator shaft. Yaw motors orient the nacelle into the wind, while blade pitch and generator torque are continuously adjusted by the turbine controller.
samples.vfa_target: Wind turbine rotational dynamics and power generation kinematics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent blade rotation speed, blades appear to stutter or change angular velocity mid-spin

questions.text: What is the typical rotational speed of a wind turbine rotor?
questions.q: What is the typical rotational speed of a wind turbine rotor?

questions.text: What controls blade angle on a wind turbine?
questions.q: What controls blade angle on a wind turbine?

questions.text: What converts low-speed rotation to high-speed rotation in a turbine?
questions.q: What converts low-speed rotation to high-speed rotation in a turbine?

questions.text: What orients the turbine nacelle into the wind?
questions.q: What orients the turbine nacelle into the wind?

samples.prompt: Analyze this FANUC robotic arm from a 45-degree orbital camera path. Describe the robot's mechanical structure, joint configuration, and how the orbital viewpoint reveals the workspace envelope and reach capabilities of the industrial manipulator.
samples.text: A FANUC industrial robot arm viewed from a 45-degree orbital path reveals its six-axis articulated structure: base rotation, shoulder and elbow pitch, and three wrist axes (roll, pitch, yaw). The orbital camera path exposes the full workspace envelope, showing how the arm can reach positions above, below, and around the work cell from its fixed base.
samples.vfa_target: Industrial robot spatial geometry and workspace kinematics
samples.failure_target: I2V model distorts robot link proportions during orbit, joint positions shift inconsistently or arm geometry warps at oblique angles

questions.text: How many axes does a typical FANUC industrial robot have?
questions.q: How many axes does a typical FANUC industrial robot have?

questions.text: What is the workspace envelope of a robot arm?
options: The volume the end-effector can reach
questions.q: What is the workspace envelope of a robot arm?
questions.answer: The volume the end-effector can reach

questions.text: What type of joint provides base rotation on a FANUC robot?
questions.q: What type of joint provides base rotation on a FANUC robot?

samples.prompt: Analyze this PCB during a lateral translation. Describe the copper trace routing, component placement, and how the camera motion reveals the continuity of trace paths across the board surface.
samples.text: During a lateral translation across a PCB, the copper trace routes maintain their routing paths as the camera pans. Surface mount components remain fixed to their pads while traces extend continuously between via junctions and component pads. The lateral motion reveals the systematic routing patterns used in multi-layer board design, with power and signal traces following distinct path conventions.
samples.vfa_target: PCB trace continuity and lateral motion fidelity
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent trace routing during lateral camera movement, traces appear to shift or disconnect mid-generation

questions.text: What routing patterns are typical in multi-layer PCB design?
options: Power and signal traces follow distinct path conventions
All traces run in one direction
questions.q: What routing patterns are typical in multi-layer PCB design?
questions.answer: Power and signal traces follow distinct path conventions

questions.text: During successful lateral camera translation on a PCB, do copper trace routes maintain their routing paths?
questions.q: During successful lateral camera translation on a PCB, do copper trace routes maintain their routing paths?

samples.prompt: Analyze the sensitivity of this aircraft's lift characteristics to angle of attack changes. Describe how small variations in pitch attitude affect the lift coefficient and overall aerodynamic performance.
samples.text: Aircraft lift sensitivity to angle of attack is a fundamental aerodynamic characteristic. The lift curve slope determines how responsive the aircraft is to pitch changes, with linear behavior in the normal operating range and a sharp loss at the critical angle of attack leading to stall.
samples.vfa_target: Aerodynamic sensitivity analysis and lift curve characterization
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to accurately represent subtle angle of attack changes and their effect on aircraft attitude

questions.text: What happens to lift when angle of attack increases in the linear range?
questions.q: What happens to lift when angle of attack increases in the linear range?

questions.text: What occurs at the critical angle of attack?
questions.q: What occurs at the critical angle of attack?

samples.prompt: Analyze the sensitivity of this spacecraft's thermal protection system to reentry heating rates. Describe how variations in reentry angle affect heat flux distribution on the vehicle surface.
samples.text: Spacecraft reentry thermal sensitivity depends on the entry angle and velocity. Steeper entries produce higher peak heating rates but shorter duration, while shallower entries spread the heat load over longer time periods. The thermal protection system must be designed to handle the worst-case heating scenario.
samples.vfa_target: Reentry thermal sensitivity and heat flux distribution analysis
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to represent thermal gradient changes across the vehicle surface during reentry angle variations

questions.text: What produces higher peak heating during reentry?
questions.q: What produces higher peak heating during reentry?

questions.text: How does a shallower reentry angle affect heat distribution?
questions.q: How does a shallower reentry angle affect heat distribution?

samples.prompt: Analyze the sensitivity of this satellite's orbital parameters to perturbation forces. Describe how small thrust variations affect the orbital elements and long-term trajectory.
samples.text: Satellite orbital sensitivity to thrust perturbations is characterized by the variational equations of orbital mechanics. Small continuous thrust forces produce gradual changes in semi-major axis, eccentricity, and inclination over multiple orbits, with sensitivity depending on the thrust direction relative to the orbital velocity vector.
samples.vfa_target: Orbital mechanics sensitivity and perturbation analysis
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent orbital trajectory when representing small perturbation effects

questions.text: What orbital element is most affected by along-track thrust?
questions.q: What orbital element is most affected by along-track thrust?

questions.text: How do small perturbation forces affect satellite orbits?
questions.q: How do small perturbation forces affect satellite orbits?

samples.prompt: Analyze the landing gear retraction mechanism of this aircraft. Describe the kinematic linkage, hydraulic actuators, and how the gear transitions from extended to stowed position during the retraction sequence.
samples.text: Aircraft landing gear retraction involves a multi-link kinematic mechanism driven by hydraulic actuators. The main gear rotates upward and inward into the wheel well, while the nose gear retracts forward. The mechanism includes drag braces, side stays, and locking links that ensure the gear is fully extended and locked before landing. During retraction, the wheel assembly must clear the door opening while maintaining structural alignment throughout the arc of motion.
samples.vfa_target: Landing gear kinematic linkage and retraction sequence
samples.failure_target: I2V model distorts landing gear linkage geometry during retraction orbit, pivot points shift inconsistently or linkage proportions warp mid-animation

questions.text: What drives the landing gear retraction mechanism?
questions.q: What drives the landing gear retraction mechanism?

questions.text: What structural members ensure the gear is locked in the extended position?
questions.q: What structural members ensure the gear is locked in the extended position?

questions.text: During an orbital camera view of landing gear retraction, do the pivot points maintain consistent spatial positions relative to the airframe?
questions.q: During an orbital camera view of landing gear retraction, do the pivot points maintain consistent spatial positions relative to the airframe?

samples.prompt: Analyze the internal compressor stages of this jet engine from an orbital camera path. Describe the blade geometry, stator-rotor arrangement, and how the multi-stage compression system achieves pressure rise.
samples.text: A jet engine axial compressor consists of alternating rows of rotating blades and stationary stators arranged along the engine axis. Each stage progressively compresses the incoming air, with blade angles and chord lengths varying along the compressor to manage the decreasing flow volume. The orbital camera path reveals how the blade rows are nested concentrically, with each rotor-stator pair designed to add a specific pressure ratio to the overall compression cycle.
samples.vfa_target: Jet engine compressor stage geometry and flow path
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent blade-to-blade spacing and stator-rotor alignment during orbital pan through the compressor section

questions.text: What is the arrangement of components in an axial compressor?
questions.q: What is the arrangement of components in an axial compressor?

questions.text: How does blade geometry change along the compressor length?
options: Angles and chords vary to manage decreasing flow volume
Blades get larger toward the rear
questions.q: How does blade geometry change along the compressor length?
questions.answer: Angles and chords vary to manage decreasing flow volume

questions.text: During an orbital view of the compressor section, does the spacing between adjacent blade rows remain consistent?
questions.q: During an orbital view of the compressor section, does the spacing between adjacent blade rows remain consistent?

samples.prompt: Analyze this commercial airliner during a nose-up pitch rotation. Describe how the aircraft's pitch attitude changes, the wing angle of attack variation, and the aerodynamic forces during the tilt maneuver.
samples.text: During a nose-up tilt, the commercial airliner rotates about its lateral axis, increasing the angle of attack of the wings. The main gear remains in contact with the runway while the nose lifts, transitioning the aircraft from ground roll to climb attitude. The wing generates increasing lift as the angle of attack rises, with the horizontal stabilizer providing a nose-down pitching moment to maintain pitch control authority throughout the rotation.
samples.vfa_target: Aircraft pitch rotation and lift generation dynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent fuselage-wing geometry during nose-up tilt, angle of attack changes appear disconnected from pitch attitude

questions.text: What axis does the aircraft rotate about during a nose-up pitch?
questions.q: What axis does the aircraft rotate about during a nose-up pitch?

questions.text: What happens to wing lift as angle of attack increases in the normal range?
questions.q: What happens to wing lift as angle of attack increases in the normal range?

questions.text: During a tilt animation, does the fuselage-wing geometric relationship remain rigid and consistent?
questions.q: During a tilt animation, does the fuselage-wing geometric relationship remain rigid and consistent?

samples.prompt: Analyze this cargo aircraft during a vertical rise. Describe how the aircraft's massive fuselage and high-wing configuration appear as the camera rises from ground level, revealing the landing gear, cargo doors, and wing structure.
samples.text: During a vertical camera rise along a cargo aircraft, the view transitions from the landing gear and lower fuselage to the high-wing configuration and engine nacelles. Cargo aircraft like the C-17 or An-124 feature a high-wing design that allows the fuselage to sit low for easy cargo loading. The vertical rise reveals the T-tail configuration, rear cargo ramp, and the relationship between wing dihedral and engine placement on the wing pylons.

samples.text: During a dolly-in toward a Boeing 747 engine nacelle, the camera reveals progressive detail of the high-bypass turbofan housing. The nacelle consists of an aerodynamic inlet lip that smooths airflow into the fan, a fan cowl that houses the large front fan, and a thrust reverser section at the rear. As the camera approaches, the panel lines, acoustic liner perforations, and the pylon mounting structure become visible, showing how the nacelle is engineered for both aerodynamic efficiency and maintenance access.

samples.prompt: Analyze this aircraft cockpit windshield during a dolly-in approach. Describe the multi-panel glass construction, frame geometry, and how the curved panels maintain optical clarity while meeting bird-strike resistance requirements.
samples.text: During a dolly-in toward an aircraft cockpit windshield, the camera reveals the multi-panel construction of the flight deck glazing. The windshield consists of multiple layers of stretched acrylic or chemically strengthened glass with interlayers for bird-strike resistance. The frame structure divides the windshield into distinct panels with heated elements to prevent ice formation. As the camera approaches, the curvature of each panel, the sealing gaskets, and the fastener pattern along the aluminum frame become progressively visible.

samples.prompt: Analyze this jet engine turbine blade array from an orbital camera path. Describe the blade geometry, cooling holes, and how the repeating lattice structure of blades creates a uniform flow path through the turbine section.
samples.text: A jet engine turbine section contains multiple rows of airfoil-shaped blades arranged in a regular circumferential pattern around the rotor disk. Each blade features internal cooling passages and surface film-cooling holes that prevent thermal damage from the hot gas path. The orbital camera view reveals how the blade array forms a repeating lattice structure, with consistent blade spacing, uniform pitch angles, and aligned trailing edges that collectively create an efficient energy extraction surface from the combustion gases.

samples.prompt: Analyze this Airbus A380 wing during an orbital camera path. Describe the wing's flexible structure, winglet design, and how the wing surface deforms under aerodynamic load as the camera orbits around it.
samples.text: The Airbus A380 wing is a large, flexible structure designed to bend upward under aerodynamic load. The wing features a high aspect ratio with raked winglets at the tips, multiple control surfaces including ailerons and flaps, and a smooth composite skin surface. During an orbital camera path, the wing's swept planform, the gradual twist from root to tip, and the surface curvature are revealed. The wing flex under load means the dihedral angle changes dynamically, creating a complex surface geometry that the camera must track consistently.

samples.prompt: Analyze this crane boom extension mechanism. Describe the telescoping sections, hydraulic cylinders, and how the boom extends kinematically while maintaining structural alignment.
samples.text: A mobile crane boom extension system consists of multiple telescoping sections that slide within each other, driven by hydraulic extension cylinders. The base section is the largest, with each successive section nesting inside the previous one. The kinematic extension sequence is controlled by synchronized hydraulic cylinders and guided by wear pads that maintain alignment between sections. As the boom extends, the moment arm increases, requiring the counterweight and outrigger system to provide stability against tipping.
samples.vfa_target: Telescoping boom kinematics and hydraulic extension sequence
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain proper nesting alignment between telescoping sections during extension, section proportions warp or overlap incorrectly

samples.prompt: Analyze this excavator arm hydraulic kinematics. Describe the boom, stick, and bucket linkage geometry, and how the hydraulic cylinders control the multi-joint articulated arm movement.
samples.text: An excavator arm consists of three main articulated segments: the boom, stick (arm), and bucket, each controlled by hydraulic cylinders. The boom cylinder raises and lowers the entire arm assembly, the stick cylinder controls the angle between boom and stick, and the bucket cylinder actuates the bucket through a linkage system that multiplies the curl force. The kinematic chain creates a planar mechanism where the bucket tip position is determined by the combined extension of all three cylinder positions, allowing the operator to trace complex digging profiles.
samples.vfa_target: Excavator arm multi-joint kinematics and hydraulic control
samples.failure_target: I2V model distorts excavator arm linkage geometry during movement, joint angles and cylinder positions become inconsistent with bucket trajectory

questions.answer: Bucket curl through a linkage system

samples.prompt: Analyze this container ship deck from an orbital camera path. Describe the container grid arrangement, cell guides, and how the repeating lattice of standardized containers creates a uniform deck surface.
samples.text: A container ship deck displays a regular lattice of ISO shipping containers arranged in bays, rows, and tiers. Containers are 20 or 40 feet long, stacked in vertical columns guided by cell guides that ensure alignment. The orbital camera view reveals the grid pattern formed by container corners, twist-lock positions, and the systematic color coding used for destination identification. The lattice structure extends across the entire deck width, with lashing bridges and securing rods maintaining the grid integrity during sea transport.

samples.prompt: Analyze the suspension bridge cable array from a panning camera. Describe the main cables, suspender cables, and how the repeating pattern of vertical hangers creates a regular lattice structure supporting the deck.
samples.text: A suspension bridge cable array consists of two main catenary cables running between towers, with vertical suspender cables hanging at regular intervals to support the bridge deck. The suspender cables form a repeating lattice pattern along the bridge length, each attached to the main cable via a cable band and to the deck via a socket assembly. The panning camera reveals how the suspender spacing is uniform, the cable diameters are consistent, and the vertical alignment creates a regular rhythm that distributes the deck load evenly to the main cables.

samples.prompt: Analyze this cargo ship hull from an orbital camera path. Describe the hull surface geometry, bow shape, and how the smooth curved surface transitions from the waterline to the deck edge.
samples.text: A cargo ship hull is a complex curved surface designed for hydrodynamic efficiency. The bow flares outward to deflect waves, the midship section has a flat bottom with bilge curves transitioning to vertical sides, and the stern narrows to accommodate the propeller and rudder. The orbital camera path reveals how the hull surface maintains continuous curvature across these transitions, with plate seams and weld lines following the surface contour. The anti-fouling paint line at the waterline marks the boundary between the underwater hull and the topside surfaces.

samples.prompt: Analyze this tower crane during a vertical camera rise. Describe the tower sections, jib, and counter-jib geometry as the camera ascends from the base to the top of the crane structure.
samples.text: During a vertical camera rise along a tower crane, the view transitions from the concrete foundation and base section through the lattice tower sections to the top-slewing unit. The tower consists of standardized lattice sections connected by high-strength bolts, creating a rigid vertical structure. At the top, the slewing unit connects the rotating jib (long arm for load lifting) to the counter-jib (short arm with concrete counterweights). The vertical rise reveals how the tower maintains consistent cross-section dimensions while the jib and counter-jib extend horizontally from the apex.

samples.prompt: Analyze this wind turbine during blade rotation. Describe the blade pitch mechanism, hub geometry, and how the three blades maintain aerodynamic balance as they rotate through the wind.
samples.text: A wind turbine's three blades rotate about a horizontal axis, with each blade connected to the hub through a pitch bearing that allows angle adjustment. The blade rotation creates a swept area that captures wind energy, with the pitch mechanism optimizing the angle of attack for different wind speeds. During rotation, the blades must maintain aerodynamic balance, with each blade having identical mass distribution and aerodynamic profiles. The hub houses the pitch actuators and rotates at 10-20 RPM, transferring torque through the main shaft to the gearbox and generator.

samples.prompt: Analyze this pump jack during its oscillating stroke. Describe the walking beam pivot, sucker rod string, and how the counterweight balances the upstroke and downstroke loads.
samples.text: A pump jack (nodding donkey) converts rotary motion from the prime mover into reciprocating linear motion through a walking beam that pivots on a central Sampson post. The horsehead at one end of the walking beam guides the polished rod and sucker rod string downhole, while the counterweight on the opposite end balances the rod weight during the stroke cycle. The oscillating motion follows a sinusoidal pattern, with the upstroke lifting the rod string and fluid column, and the downstroke allowing the traveling valve to open as fluid fills the pump barrel.
samples.vfa_target: Pump jack oscillating mechanism and counterbalance dynamics
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent pivot geometry and counterweight balance during oscillation, walking beam angle or rod position becomes inconsistent

questions.q: What is the purpose of the counterweight on the pump jack?
questions.answer: Balances rod weight during stroke cycle

The lateral view reveals the roller spacing, belt tracking mechanism, and how the support frame maintains belt alignment. samples.prompt: Analyze this delta-arm robot during a pick-and-place operation. Describe the parallel kinematic structure, three arm linkages, and how the end effector maintains orientation while the arms move in coordinated trajectories.
samples.text: A delta robot consists of three parallelogram arms connected to a common base platform, with all three arms jointly controlling the position of a central end-effector platform. Each arm is driven by a rotary actuator at the base, with the parallelogram structure ensuring the end-effector remains horizontal throughout the workspace. During pick-and-place operations, the three arms move in coordinated trajectories to achieve high-speed positioning, with the kinematic structure providing high stiffness and low moving mass. The camera reveals how arm positions change simultaneously to trace complex paths.

samples.prompt: Analyze this quadruped robot during a walking gait cycle. Describe the leg linkages, hip-knee joint angles, and how the four legs coordinate in a trot gait pattern to maintain stable locomotion.
samples.text: A quadruped robot walks using coordinated leg movements that follow a trot gait pattern, where diagonal leg pairs move in synchrony. Each leg consists of three segments: hip mount, upper leg, and lower leg, connected by hip and knee revolute joints. The gait cycle involves stance phase (foot on ground, bearing load) and swing phase (foot lifted, advancing forward). The camera reveals how the four legs maintain a stable support polygon throughout the gait, with joint angles adjusting in real-time to keep the body level and maintain forward momentum.
samples.vfa_target: Quadruped robot gait kinematics and leg coordination
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent leg linkage proportions and joint angle relationships during gait cycle, legs appear to lengthen or shorten inconsistently

samples.prompt: Analyze this robotic arm during a welding operation. Describe the torch trajectory, joint coordination, and how the six-axis arm maintains a consistent welding angle along a complex seam path.
samples.text: A robotic welding arm follows a programmed seam path while maintaining a consistent torch angle, travel speed, and contact-tip-to-work distance. The six-axis articulated arm coordinates all joints simultaneously to trace complex 3D seam geometries, with the wrist axes providing the fine angular adjustments needed for proper weld puddle orientation. The camera reveals how the arm smoothly transitions between seam segments, with each joint rotating at calculated speeds to maintain the TCP (tool center point) velocity along the programmed path.
samples.vfa_target: Robotic welding arm trajectory tracking and joint coordination
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent torch trajectory and joint angle relationships during welding path, arm geometry becomes inconsistent with TCP position

questions.text: What does TCP stand for in robotic welding?
questions.q: What does TCP stand for in robotic welding?

questions.q: What wrist axes provide for proper weld puddle orientation?

samples.prompt: Analyze this FANUC robot arm from an orbital camera path. Describe the arm's cylindrical and spherical joint housings, cable routing, and how the smooth industrial surfaces maintain consistent geometry around the arm.
samples.text: A FANUC industrial robot arm features cylindrical joint housings at each axis, with smooth painted surfaces that enclose the motors, gearboxes, and encoders. The orbital camera path reveals how the arm segments transition between joint housings through tapered link sections, with external cable dress packs routing along the arm exterior. The surface geometry includes mounting flanges, status indicator covers, and model identification plates integrated into the smooth housing surfaces. As the camera orbits, the consistent cylindrical geometry of each joint housing and the linear geometry of the link sections must maintain their manufactured precision.
samples.vfa_target: Robot arm surface geometry and joint housing consistency during orbit
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain cylindrical joint housing geometry during orbit, housing surfaces appear faceted or link sections distort between joints

questions.text: What shape are the joint housings on a FANUC robot arm?
questions.q: What shape are the joint housings on a FANUC robot arm?

questions.q: What runs along the exterior of the robot arm?

questions.q: During an orbital view, do the cylindrical joint housings maintain smooth, consistent geometry without faceting?

samples.prompt: Analyze this robotic end effector during a dolly-in approach. Describe the gripper fingers, pneumatic actuators, and sensor integration as the camera moves closer to the tooling.
samples.text: During a dolly-in toward a robotic end effector, the camera reveals the parallel gripper mechanism with its two opposing fingers driven by a pneumatic rotary actuator. The fingers feature replaceable rubber pads for grip compliance, with embedded force sensors providing feedback for delicate part handling. The effector body houses the pneumatic valves, tubing connections, and signal cables that interface with the robot controller. As the camera approaches, the precision machined finger guides, the actuator mounting bolts, and the sensor wiring become progressively visible, showing the compact integration of mechanical, pneumatic, and sensing components.
samples.vfa_target: End effector mechanical detail and component integration during approach
samples.failure_target: I2V model loses end effector mechanical precision during dolly-in, finger alignment or actuator geometry distorts at close range

questions.q: What typically drives a parallel gripper end effector?

questions.q: Why do gripper fingers have replaceable rubber pads?

questions.text: During a dolly-in, does the end effector maintain precise finger alignment and actuator geometry?
questions.q: During a dolly-in, does the end effector maintain precise finger alignment and actuator geometry?

samples.prompt: Analyze this humanoid robot arm-to-body connection from an orbital camera path. Describe the shoulder joint kinematics, torso surface geometry, and how the arm's kinematic chain integrates with the body's surface topology.
samples.text: A humanoid robot's shoulder joint connects the arm kinematic chain to the torso surface through a multi-degree-of-freedom joint that mimics human shoulder anatomy. The orbital camera path reveals how the upper arm segment emerges from the spherical shoulder housing, with the torso surface providing a smooth contour that encloses the battery, computing hardware, and structural frame. The arm-to-body interface involves three rotational axes (pitch, roll, yaw) that allow the arm to reach positions above, beside, and in front of the body. The surface topology of the torso transitions from flat chest panels to curved shoulder housings, creating a complex surface that the arm kinematic chain must interface with cleanly.
samples.vfa_target: Humanoid robot arm-body interface and surface-kinematic integration
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent arm-body geometric relationship during orbit, shoulder joint appears to detach from torso or surface transitions become discontinuous

questions.text: How many rotational degrees of freedom does a humanoid shoulder typically have?
questions.q: How many rotational degrees of freedom does a humanoid shoulder typically have?

options: Battery, computing hardware, and structural frame
questions.q: What components are enclosed within the humanoid robot's torso?
questions.answer: Battery, computing hardware, and structural frame

questions.text: During an orbital view, does the arm-to-body interface maintain a clean geometric connection without gaps or overlaps?
questions.q: During an orbital view, does the arm-to-body interface maintain a clean geometric connection without gaps or overlaps?

samples.prompt: Analyze this BGA (Ball Grid Array) solder ball array from an orbital camera path. Describe the regular ball pitch, ball diameter consistency, and how the grid pattern provides electrical connections between the IC package and PCB.
samples.text: A BGA solder ball array consists of hundreds or thousands of solder spheres arranged in a regular grid pattern on the underside of an IC package. Each ball is typically 0.3-0.6mm in diameter with a pitch of 0.4-1.0mm, providing both electrical connection and mechanical attachment to the PCB. The orbital camera view reveals the regular lattice structure formed by the ball array, with consistent ball diameter, uniform pitch in both X and Y directions, and perfect spherical geometry of each ball. The grid pattern ensures reliable solder joints when the package is reflow-soldered to the PCB pads.
samples.vfa_target: BGA solder ball array lattice consistency and geometric uniformity
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain uniform ball spacing and spherical geometry across the BGA array during orbital view, balls appear to shift or deform inconsistently

questions.q: What does BGA stand for?

questions.q: During an orbital view of the BGA array, is the ball-to-ball spacing uniform in both X and Y directions?

samples.prompt: Analyze this via hole grid in a multi-layer PCB from an orbital camera path. Describe the via hole diameter, pitch, and how the regular array of plated-through holes provides vertical interconnection between PCB layers.
samples.text: A via hole grid in a multi-layer PCB consists of an array of small plated-through holes that provide electrical connections between different copper layers. Each via hole is drilled mechanically or with a laser, then electroplated with copper to create a conductive barrel. The orbital camera view reveals the regular lattice pattern of via holes, with consistent diameter (typically 0.2-0.3mm for microvias), uniform pitch, and circular cross-section. The grid pattern follows the PCB design rules for minimum spacing, with the via array serving as the vertical interconnection backbone of the multi-layer board.
samples.vfa_target: Via hole grid lattice geometry and plating consistency
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain uniform via hole diameter and circular cross-section across the grid during orbital view, holes appear to distort or spacing varies

questions.q: What is the typical diameter of microvias in a PCB?

questions.q: What process creates the conductive path inside a via hole?

questions.text: During an orbital view of the via grid, is the hole diameter uniform and circular across the entire array?
questions.q: During an orbital view of the via grid, is the hole diameter uniform and circular across the entire array?

samples.prompt: Analyze this wire bonding process during oscillation. Describe the gold wire arc, capillary tool, and how the ball-stitch bonding creates interconnections between the IC die and lead frame.
samples.text: During wire bonding oscillation, a gold or aluminum wire is fed through a capillary tool that creates ball bonds on the IC die bond pads and stitch bonds on the lead frame. The wire forms a characteristic arc (loop) between the two bond points, with the loop height and shape controlled by the capillary trajectory. The oscillating motion involves the capillary descending to create a ball bond on the die pad, rising to form the wire loop, traveling to the lead frame position, and descending to create a stitch bond. Each bond cycle takes approximately 15-50 milliseconds.
samples.vfa_target: Wire bonding oscillation kinematics and loop formation
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain consistent wire loop geometry during oscillation, loop height or wire path appears discontinuous between bond points

questions.text: What material is commonly used for wire bonding?
questions.q: What material is commonly used for wire bonding?

questions.q: What creates the wire loop shape during bonding?

questions.q: During wire bonding oscillation, does the wire loop maintain a smooth, continuous arc between bond points?

samples.prompt: Analyze this IC die surface during a dolly-in approach. Describe the active circuit regions, bond pad array, and passivation layer as the camera moves closer to the silicon die.
samples.text: During a dolly-in toward an IC die surface, the camera reveals the microscopic landscape of the integrated circuit. The die surface features a glassivation (passivation) layer that protects the underlying circuitry, with bond pad openings at the periphery for wire bonding connections. The active circuit regions show patterns of metal interconnect layers, with different colors indicating different metal layers (typically aluminum or copper). As the camera approaches, the regular grid of standard cells in the digital logic area, the analog circuit blocks, and the ESD protection structures around the bond pads become progressively visible.
samples.vfa_target: IC die surface circuit layout and passivation detail during approach
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain circuit pattern consistency during dolly-in, metal interconnect patterns or bond pad geometry distorts at close range

questions.q: During a dolly-in, does the die surface maintain consistent circuit pattern geometry without visual artifacts?

samples.prompt: Analyze this silicon wafer surface from an orbital camera path. Describe the die layout pattern, scribe lines, and how the regular grid of individual die sites covers the circular wafer surface.
samples.text: A silicon wafer surface contains a regular grid of identical die sites separated by scribe lines (streets) where the wafer will be diced into individual chips. The orbital camera path reveals how the rectangular die pattern fills the circular wafer, with partial die sites at the wafer edge. Each die site contains the complete IC circuit pattern, with test structures placed in the scribe lines for process monitoring. The wafer surface has a characteristic specular finish from the CMP (chemical-mechanical planarization) process, with the die grid creating a repeating pattern that covers the entire wafer area.
samples.vfa_target: Wafer surface die grid pattern and scribe line consistency during orbit
samples.failure_target: I2V model fails to maintain uniform die grid spacing and scribe line consistency during orbital view, die boundaries shift or grid alignment drifts across the wafer
